questions.question: What does an inode store in a Unix-like file system?
answers: File metadata, size, permissions, and block pointers, but not the file name
The human-readable file name and directory path string
The literal plain-text contents of the target file
The encryption keys used for secure network transmissions

questions.question: What structural pattern does a Hard Link follow compared to a Symbolic Link?
answers: Hard links delete the original file target when broken; symbolic links do not
A Hard Link points directly to the same inode; a Symbolic Link points to a path string
Hard links require administrative privileges to execute
Symbolic links can only reference binary files; hard links handle folders

questions.question: What component maps file paths to matching inode numbers in a file system directory?
answers: Directory entries (dentries)
File allocation tables (FAT)
Virtual file layers (VFS)
Master boot records (MBR)

questions.question: What is a major advantage of a journaling file system like ext4?
answers: It speeds up graphics card rendering rates
It records changes in a log to prevent corruption during sudden power losses
It compresses text files to half their initial disk sector allocations
It encrypts all user credentials automatically

questions.question: What state does a system enter when it spends more time swapping pages than executing instructions?
answers: Paging
Thrashing
Fragmenting
Starvation

questions.question: What is the main difference between internal and external fragmentation?
answers: External happens inside kernel space; internal happens in user space
Internal occurs inside fixed allocated blocks; external occurs between variable blocks
External happens on hard drives; internal happens inside CPU registers
Internal is fixed by defragmentation; external requires virtual memory flushes

questions.question: What characterizes a monolithic kernel architecture?
answers: All operating system services run inside a single privileged kernel space
OS services are moved to separate user-space modules
It runs exclusively on virtual machines
It eliminates context switching overhead entirely

questions.question: What happens during a hardware interrupt?
answers: The active process is terminated and marked as a zombie
Virtual memory maps flush physical hard drive tracks
The CPU suspends its execution stream to run an interrupt service routine
The operating system completely reboots its kernel

questions.question: What privilege layer does the operating system kernel run in on modern CPUs?
answers: Hypervisor mode
Safe mode
Kernel mode (Ring 0)
User mode (Ring 3)

questions.question: What is a major advantage of a microkernel compared to a monolithic design?
answers: Faster execution performance speeds
Lower context switching overhead costs
Higher system stability and isolation of corrupted drivers
Smaller total storage size footprint
